name: Fonte Benémola Trail
region: Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola
description: Jog the easy 2.6-mile Fonte Benémola Trail, a peaceful route through a Protected Landscape with natural springs and lush vegetation.
region_intro: Jogging routes in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola traverse a diverse landscape in the Barrocal region of the Algarve. The area is characterized by the Ribeira da Menalva river, which sustains a dense riparian gallery with unique tree species. Away from the river, the terrain transitions to hillsides adorned with holm oaks, cork oaks, and aromatic plants, with some limestone-rich slopes and schist landscapes. This protected area offers a mix of riverside paths, wooded sections, and varied elevations suitable for running. Best jogging routes in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola The most popular jogging route is Fonte Benémola — circular route (PR16 LLE), a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes 27 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Benémola stream, offering a peaceful run through the riparian gallery. Another top favourite among local runners is Source of the Benémola Stream loop from Paisagem Protegida Local da Fonte Benémola, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This route includes varied terrain with some elevation gain, providing a good workout while exploring the protected landscape. Local runners also love the Negro – Cepo loop from Querença, an 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through hills and rural scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Jogging in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola is defined by riverside paths, wooded areas, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging runs with significant elevation. The routes in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 5 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola?
A: There are over &lt;em&gt;25&lt;/em&gt; running routes available in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy riverside paths to more challenging trails through the hills.

Q: What makes jogging in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola unique?
A: Jogging here offers a unique blend of natural beauty and historical interest. The routes traverse diverse landscapes, including the lush riparian gallery along the Ribeira da Menalva, typical Barrocal scenery with cork oaks and aromatic plants, and even schist landscapes. You'll also encounter remnants of ancient Arab-inspired hydraulic structures, adding a historical dimension to your run.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy running options perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420003820/fonte-benemola-circular-route-pr16-lle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Benémola — circular route (PR16 LLE)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.2 km (2.6 miles) trail that follows the Benémola stream, offering a peaceful run through the riparian gallery. The region also features a 4.5 km pedestrian route specifically designed for observing flora and fauna.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while running?
A: You'll encounter a rich variety of natural features. The Ribeira da Menalva river, sustained by springs like "O Olho" and Fonte Benémola, creates a dense riparian gallery with unique trees such as willows, ash, and poplars. Away from the river, you'll see hillsides with holm oaks, cork oaks, and aromatic plants like rosemary and lavender. The area is also home to 303 plant species and 288 fauna species, including otters and diverse birdlife.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The landscape is dotted with fascinating historical elements, particularly related to water management. You can discover ancient Arab-inspired hydraulic structures like norias (waterwheels), water mills, weirs, and millstreams. There's also an old lime kiln near some trailheads, offering a glimpse into past local industries. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Benémola Stream&lt;/a&gt; itself, a key highlight of the area.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the protected landscape?
A: Yes, the Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola is well-suited for circular running. Many trails, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420003820/fonte-benemola-circular-route-pr16-lle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Benémola — circular route (PR16 LLE)&lt;/a&gt;, are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Fonte Benémola?
A: The running routes in Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 5 reviews. Runners often praise the peaceful and bucolic setting, the well-maintained trails, and the diverse scenery that includes riverside paths and wooded areas.

Q: Can I find challenging running routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging workout, there are routes with significant elevation. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21823232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Negro – Cepo loop from Querença&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.9 km (8.6 miles) trail that leads through hills and rural scenery, featuring substantial elevation changes.

Q: Are there places to rest or have a picnic along the running trails?
A: Yes, designated picnic areas are available, particularly by the river. These spots provide ideal locations for runners to take a break, refuel, and relax amidst the natural beauty of the protected landscape. You can find a specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3904328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River at Fonte Benémola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Fonte Benémola?
A: The Paisagem Protegida Local Da Fonte Benémola is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant flora. Even in summer, the consistent water flow from the springs helps maintain a cooler microclimate along the riverbanks, making it suitable for early morning or late afternoon runs. Winter also offers a unique, tranquil experience.

Q: Is there an opportunity for birdwatching while jogging?
A: Absolutely. The protected area boasts an impressive biodiversity, including a wide variety of bird species. An ornithological station is present, providing excellent opportunities for birdwatching enthusiasts to observe the diverse avian life while enjoying their run.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails?
A: While the region is a natural park, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and flora, especially given the area's rich biodiversity. Always check local signage for specific regulations regarding pets on trails.
tips: A Rocky single track route that slightly extends the existing trail for a more exciting and adventurous hike. Suitable for anyone with steady feet, it’s a beautiful way to explore the caves and sloping hills around the river valley.

The Fonte Benémola is a great destination for walking, hiking and picnicking.

Fruit and vegetable gardens, as well as the typical Barrocal landscape with holm, kermes and cork oaks and hills covered with rock roses line the path.
Down by the stream the vegetation becomes denser. There are said to be 300 plant species here - some that are not otherwise found in the Algarve
Halfway along the route of the seven sources you will pass a very nice picnic area; it's nice and cool along the water in summer

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Fonte Benémola Trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is available near the trailhead. The trail research mentions a dirt road section just after the car park, indicating a designated parking area at the start of the route.

faqs.question: Is the Fonte Benémola Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it's located within the Fonte Benémola Local Protected Landscape, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. However, the spring and autumn months offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Even in summer, the presence of abundant water from the springs creates a lush environment, but it can still be warm. Visiting outside of peak heat is recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Fonte Benémola Local Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Fonte Benémola Local Protected Landscape or its trails. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is considered easy and suitable for most fitness levels, including beginners and families. While the full circular route has some slight inclines and rocky sections, there's also an option for a shorter, easier linear route that avoids the more challenging parts.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. The initial section leading to the springs is largely level ground. The full circular route includes some dirt roads, inclines, and sections with loose stones and tree roots, but it remains generally easy. It traverses woodlands, meadows, and traditional farmland.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting features, including the Fonte Benémola Local Protected Landscape itself, the Source of the Benémola Stream, and ancient hydraulic structures like watermills and lime kilns. The area is known for its lush vegetation and abundant water, a rarity in the region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, which is approximately 4.4 kilometers (2.73 miles), typically takes about 1 hour and 30 minutes to complete at a jogging pace, depending on your speed and stops.

faqs.question: Is there water available or facilities along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is notable for its abundant natural water from the springs. There is also a Picnic Area by the River at Fonte Benémola, which offers a place to rest and enjoy the surroundings.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers 100% of the official 'Fonte Benémola' trail. It also overlaps significantly with other paths, covering 93% of the 'Via Algarviana - Ligação 2: Loulé - Salir' and 41% of the '7 Fontes' trail.

faqs.question: Is there an option for a shorter or easier route?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you prefer a less challenging experience, there is an option for a shorter, easier linear route. This alternative avoids the more inclined and rocky sections of the full circular path, making it ideal for a more relaxed stroll or a quick jog to the springs and back.

waypoints.name: Picnic Area by the River at Fonte Benémola
